original_image_tags: Aged, off-white envelope, torn and frayed at edges, with two punched holes on the left side. Postmarked in purple ink. Handwritten address in blue ink. Redacted return address in upper left corner. Purple 3-cent United States postage stamp featuring Thomas Jefferson in the upper right.

elements.description: A photograph of the back of a white envelope. It features a purple 3-cent United States Postage stamp in the top right corner with a profile portrait and the text 'UNITED STATES POSTAGE' and '3 CENTS 3'. A circular postmark stamp reads 'WOODSTOCK ILL. JUL 7 7 AM 1947'. There is a rectangular blue ink stamp in the top left corner that is heavily crossed out and illegible. Handwritten cursive address text in the center reads 'Mr. J. Edgar Hoover. F.B.I. Washington, D.C.'. The envelope has a torn right edge, two black punch holes on the left side, and visible fold creases.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the visual elements of the envelope: a purple 3-cent US postage stamp, a postmark, handwritten address, a redacted or illegible top-left mark, two punch holes on the left, and a torn right edge.
